Event/Report: 1.1 – Safety events by User (by station, ranked)
Required Data Points:
• Station code/name
• Employee ID (and display name)
• Event type (Harsh Acceleration, Harsh Brake, Harsh Turn, Overspeed, Shock/Impact, Seatbelt)
• Event timestamp (event start; optional end/duration)
• Asset ID and Asset Type
• GPS location (lat/long) at event time
• Event severity metrics (e.g., g‑force, delta‑V, max speed, turning rate)
• Speed at time of event (km/h or mph)
• Seatbelt state at event time (on/off)
• Ignition state at event time
• Event/telemetry source record ID
What's potentially an issue/challenge:
What do we get for Asset_id? The event messages seem to include "asset_id" only, not type or class.

Need to confirm if seatbelt state is available and in which event/topic.

Need to confirm if event severity metrics are available.
From Notes/vendor::
GSE name is what we display, Asset_ID is what we want, can be same as GSE name. 

Seat belt state does not come through today in any event based message. One customer asked 2 weeks ago so they will add it. State of When was engaged and state of when sit on seat. For sure will happen in 2026.

Event is yes/no, no severity detail.

Event/Report: 1.4 – Attempts by unqualified drivers to start assets (by station, by employee, incl. asset type)
Required Data Points:
• Station code/name
• Employee ID and qualifications held (e.g., Baseline, CLS/CLW, LTL/LCL/WTL, ASL/ASM)
• Required qualification for asset / asset type
• Asset ID and Asset Type
• Attempt timestamp
• Attempt outcome (blocked/allowed)
• Reason code (e.g., access control not active, shunted, insufficient qualification)
• Access control device ID (badge/FOB/terminal)
• Authentication result / error message
What's potentially an issue/challenge:
With regards to asset type/class, what is in the message? Do we have to make our own conversion table to figure this out?

What will we get for reason?
From Notes/vendor::
Brand, model, year, station, these can be pushed in the event based message yes.

Event will have who tried it, which gse, where it was, time stamp. If we have it in the system, we will have the name (eg. Swissport tries to start it).

Event/Report: 1.5 – Daily Inspection Compliance (motorized assets)
Required Data Points:
• Station code/name
• Asset master list (Asset ID, Asset Type, Motorized flag, in-service status)
• Inspection schedule (daily) per asset
• Inspection completion record (timestamp, inspector ID, pass/fail)
• Missed inspection indicator per asset/day
• Out-of-service status and effective dates (to exclude)
What's potentially an issue/challenge: Do we get an event for each inspection completed?
From Notes/vendor:: There is an event when inspection is completed and an event when the checklist fails and which qusetion failed.

Event/Report: 2.1 – GSE Asset Utilization Rate (monthly)
Required Data Points:
• Asset ID and Asset Type
• Station code/name
• State timeline with timestamps (e.g., In use, Is moving, Is working, Idle, Ignition on)
• Time bucket/calendar (date, week, month)
• Operating hours meter (if available)

Event/Report: 2.2 – GSE Asset Availability Rate (live data; weekly reporting)
Required Data Points:
• Asset ID and Asset Type
• Station code/name
• State timeline with timestamps (Available/Serviceable vs In maintenance/Repair Requested/Is Halted/Restricted)
• Charging state and SoC (to decide if considered available)
• Start/end times of unavailability windows
• Calendar period for aggregation
What's potentially an issue/challenge:
TEST CAN REPORT OPTION

- EV charging discounted, charge < 10% discounted?

If unit is CHARGING, can we tell?  Averist API data?? Or just Geofence?
From Notes/vendor::
Charging: The vehicle is in the loading area.
Complementary status
Restricted: This is linked to OSHA Checklist. When a person is answering engine on questions, or has knowingly decided not to answer them. The vehicle is in use restricted (it emits a sound and is in turtle mode)
Low battery: energy level (diesel or electric) on GSE is under 40% (when fuel level or State of Charge is configured) 
Charging: The equipment is being charged
Charged: The equipment is charged
